articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha hit with heat wave: Nine places record temperature above 40°c
On Friday, the mercury soared sharply across the Odisha with maximum temperature above 40 degrees Celcius in as many as nine places of the state.
Bhubaneswar: Odisha is sweltering under high temperature for the past few days. On Friday, the mercury soared sharply across the Odisha with maximum temperature above 40°c in as many as nine places of the state.
As per the IMD data, the Western Odisha town of Jharsuguda was the hottest place with highest maximum temperature of 41.6 Degree C in the state.
Angul recorded the second highest maximum temperature of 40.7 Degree C, followed by 40.5 Degree Celsius in Titilagarh in Balangir district and Bhadrak.
The maximum temperature was 40.4 Degree C at Hirakud in Sambalpur, 40.3 Degree C at Bolangir and 40 Degree C at Talcher in Angul district, Paralakhemundi in Gajapati district and Sambalpur.
Altogether four places—Rourkela, Sundargarh, Chandbali and Baripada—recorded the maximum temperature between 39 Degree C and 40 Degree C.
The twin cities recorded temperature of 38.4 in Cuttack and 38.7 degrees in Bhubaneswar.
The temperature was 2.2 degrees higher than normal. Under the influence of Kalabaisakhi, the temperature will drop by 2 to 3 degrees in the next 2 days.
The Bhubaneswar Regional Meteorological Center has issued a yellow warning for Kalabaisakhi in most districts of the state until the April 8. Yellow warning has been issued for Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore and Bhadrak districts today.

articles.title: IMD issues yellow alert for heat, thunderstorms across Odisha; rain likely over several districts - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow warning (be aware) for multiple districts of Odisha over the next six days, forecasting a mix of heatwave-like conditions, thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds, along with scattered rainfall activity.
Day 1 (April 4 morning)
Light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely in districts including Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Angul, Boudh, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, Nayagarh, Dhenkanal, Nuapada, Cuttack, Nabarangpur and Balangir, while the rest of Odisha is expected to remain dry.
Heat Alert: Hot and humid conditions are likely in Balasore, Bhadrak, Kendrapara, and Jagatsinghpur.
Thunderstorm Alert (40–50 kmph winds): Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Kalahandi, Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, Kandhamal.
Moderate winds (30–40 kmph): Nuapada, Nabarangpur, Balangir.
Day 2 (April 4–5)
Rain and thunderstorms are expected mainly over Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Rayagada, Koraput, and Malkangiri.
Yellow warning: Thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds (30–40 kmph) in these districts.
Day 3 (April 5–6)
Rain activity will expand to Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Nuapada.
Yellow warning: Thunderstorms with lightning and winds up to 30–40 kmph in these regions.
Day 4 (April 6–7)
Rain or thunderstorms are likely across several parts of Odisha.
Stronger winds (40–50 kmph): Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj, Ganjam, Gajapati.
Moderate winds (30–40 kmph): Puri, Khordha, Rayagada, Malkangiri, Koraput, Nayagarh, Nabarangpur, Dhenkanal, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, Boudh, Angul.
Day 5 (April 7–8)
Thunderstorm activity intensifies across northern, coastal, and southern districts.
High wind alert (40–50 kmph): Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Nabarangpur, Kalahandi, Kandhamal.
Moderate winds (30–40 kmph): Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, Bargarh, Sambalpur, Deogarh, Angul, Dhenkanal, Sonepur, Boudh, Balangir, Koraput, Malkangiri, Nuapada.
Day 6 (April 8–9)
Light to moderate rain or thundershowers are likely in Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj, Sundargarh, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati, with dry weather elsewhere.
